mcqs.question: Which header file is required for file handling in C++?
mcqs.explanation: The <fstream> header provides ifstream, ofstream, and fstream classes for file operations in C++.

mcqs.question: What does ifstream stand for?
mcqs.explanation: ifstream is the input file stream class used for reading from files.

mcqs.question: Which mode is used to append data to an existing file?
mcqs.explanation: ios::app (append mode) positions the pointer at the end before each write, preserving existing content.

mcqs.question: What is the default file open mode for ofstream?
mcqs.explanation: ofstream defaults to ios::out | ios::trunc, creating new file or truncating existing one.

mcqs.question: Which function is used to read an entire line from a file?
mcqs.explanation: getline(ifstream, string) reads an entire line including spaces until newline or EOF.

mcqs.question: What does the is_open() function return?
mcqs.explanation: is_open() returns a boolean - true if file opened successfully, false otherwise.

mcqs.question: Which file mode positions the pointer at the end when file is opened?
mcqs.explanation: ios::ate (at end) positions the pointer at end of file but allows writing anywhere.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of ios::binary mode?

options.text: Opens file for binary data (no text formatting)

mcqs.correctAnswer: Opens file for binary data (no text formatting)
mcqs.explanation: ios::binary opens file in binary mode, disabling text mode translations like newline conversion.

mcqs.question: Which operator is used to write data to a file using ofstream?
mcqs.explanation: The insertion operator (<<) is used with ofstream to write data to files, same as cout.

mcqs.question: What happens when you try to open a non-existent file with ifstream in default mode?

options.text: Sets failbit, file cannot be read

mcqs.correctAnswer: Sets failbit, file cannot be read
mcqs.explanation: ifstream with default ios::in mode fails to open non-existent files and sets failbit.

mcqs.question: Which function positions the get pointer (read position) in a file?
mcqs.explanation: seekg() positions the get pointer for reading; seekp() positions the put pointer for writing.

mcqs.question: What does tellg() return?
mcqs.explanation: tellg() returns the current position of the get pointer as a streampos object.

mcqs.question: Which class allows both reading and writing on the same file?
mcqs.explanation: fstream provides both input and output capabilities for reading and writing simultaneously.

mcqs.question: What is the correct way to open a file for writing in text mode?
mcqs.explanation: ofstream with ios::out opens file for writing in text mode (default).

mcqs.question: What does ios::trunc mode do to an existing file?

options.text: Deletes content and creates new file

mcqs.correctAnswer: Deletes content and creates new file
mcqs.explanation: ios::trunc (truncate) deletes existing file content, creating an empty file.

mcqs.question: Which method closes the file stream?
mcqs.explanation: The close() method closes the file and releases associated resources.

mcqs.question: What is read() function used for?

options.text: Reading raw bytes into character array

mcqs.correctAnswer: Reading raw bytes into character array
mcqs.explanation: read(char*, n) reads n bytes of raw data into a character buffer.

mcqs.question: What is the return type of eof() function?
mcqs.explanation: eof() returns true when end-of-file has been reached, false otherwise.

mcqs.question: Which operator is used to read data from ifstream?
mcqs.explanation: The extraction operator (>>) is used with ifstream to read data, same as cin.

mcqs.question: What happens if you don't close a file in C++?
mcqs.back: The file is automatically closed when the file stream object goes out of scope and its destructor is called
